title_en: Marriage and Transactions in Al-Muqaddimah al-Hadramiyyah
content_en:
Ba-Fadl al-Hadhrami's chapter on nikah (marriage) in Al-Muqaddimah presents the Shafi'i school's essential conditions for a valid marriage in concise form. Marriage is defined as a contract legalizing the enjoyment between the spouses through the specific formula of offer and acceptance, and establishing the rights and obligations of both parties.

The conditions of a valid Shafi'i marriage contract are: a sane, adult male guardian (wali) — either the father, grandfather, or other authorized male relative — the groom, two adult male Muslim witnesses, and the proper offer and acceptance using words that clearly convey marriage. Unlike the Hanafi school, the Shafi'i school requires the wali's participation for validity, not merely for recommendation. A woman who contracts her own marriage without a wali has no valid marriage in the Shafi'i school.

The mahr (dower) is obligatory upon the husband — a gift of wealth from the groom to the bride established by Quranic command and prophetic practice. No minimum is set in the Shafi'i school. If the marriage is contracted without specifying a mahr, the marriage is valid and the wife is entitled to mahr al-mithl (the customary dower for women of her family and status).

Ba-Fadl mentions briefly the categories of women whom a man may not marry: those permanently prohibited (mothers, daughters, sisters, aunts, and their daughters by blood, milk, or marriage) and those temporarily prohibited (wife's sister while married to her, a fifth wife simultaneously, a woman in another man's 'iddah). These prohibitions are established by explicit Quranic verses (4:22–24) and prophetic tradition.

For commercial transactions, Al-Muqaddimah covers the essentials of valid sale: a seller and buyer with legal capacity, a specific known item, a known price, and a proper offer and acceptance. Sales forbidden due to gharar (uncertainty) — such as selling fish still in the water or birds in the sky — are mentioned as impermissible based on the prophetic prohibition of gharar transactions (Muslim).

The prohibition of riba is stated clearly as one of the major prohibitions in Islamic commercial law, grounded in the Quranic verses of Surah al-Baqarah (2:275–279) which declare war from Allah and His Messenger upon those who persist in riba after being warned. Ba-Fadl notes that riba is not merely a financial matter but a moral one — it constitutes exploitation of need and is fundamentally contrary to the Islamic values of justice and brotherhood.

Ba-Fadl closes Al-Muqaddimah with a reminder that the knowledge of fiqh — of what is permitted and what is prohibited — is itself one of the most important religious obligations. The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: 'Seeking knowledge is an obligation upon every Muslim' (Ibn Majah). Understanding one's religious duties is the prerequisite for fulfilling them, and the student who masters this primer has taken the essential first step on the path of Islamic scholarship and practice.
title_id: Perkawinan dan Transaksi dalam Al-Muqaddimah al-Hadramiyyah
content_id:
Bab nikah (perkawinan) karya Ba-Fadhl al-Hadrami dalam Al-Muqaddimah menyajikan syarat-syarat esensial pernikahan yang sah menurut mazhab Syafi'i dalam bentuk yang ringkas. Pernikahan didefinisikan sebagai akad yang menghalalkan hubungan antara suami istri dengan lafal ijab dan qabul yang spesifik, dan memiliki lima rukun: dua calon mempelai, wali, dua saksi, dan sighat (lafal akad).

Persyaratan wali dalam mazhab Syafi'i bersifat mutlak — tidak ada pernikahan yang sah tanpa wali bagi perempuan. Urutan wali yang berhak menikahkan adalah: ayah, kakek dari pihak ayah, saudara laki-laki kandung, saudara laki-laki seayah, paman dari pihak ayah, dan seterusnya. Wali hakim (qadhi) dapat menggantikan wali nasab dalam kondisi-kondisi tertentu, seperti ketika wali nasab tidak ada atau menolak menikahkan tanpa alasan yang sah.

Syarat-syarat dua saksi juga dipaparkan dengan ringkas: keduanya harus laki-laki, Muslim, baligh, berakal, adil (tidak fasik), dan mendengar serta memahami akad yang diucapkan. Persyaratan saksi ini lebih ketat dari beberapa mazhab lain dalam beberapa aspek.

Bagian muamalat (transaksi komersial) mencakup pokok-pokok hukum jual beli: definisi, rukun-rukunnya (penjual, pembeli, barang, dan harga, serta sighat), dan syarat-syarat keabsahannya. Larangan riba dan gharar (ketidakpastian berlebihan) disebutkan sebagai prinsip dasar yang membatasi jenis-jenis transaksi yang diperbolehkan. Meskipun ringkas, bagian ini memberikan fondasi yang cukup untuk memahami prinsip-prinsip dasar muamalat dalam pandangan mazhab Syafi'i.
